wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AITrendTracker project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as a real-time AI-powered trend discovery system that aggregates social media, news, and cultural phenomena data. Document the full-stack architecture combining React Native mobile application with Node.js/Express backend. Describe the core value proposition of providing predictive intelligence about emerging trends through AI analysis. Include technology stack summary covering React Native 0.84.1, Redux Toolkit, Socket.IO, MongoDB, and AI/ML integrations. Provide high-level system architecture overview showing data flow from external sources through AI processing to real-time user delivery. Address the target audience including trend researchers, marketers, and general users interested in cultural intelligence.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Native Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the React Native application structure and configuration. Explain the project layout starting from App.tsx as the main entry point, through index.js for native module registration, and app.json for React Native CLI configuration. Document the TypeScript configuration, Babel transformation setup, and Metro bundler configuration. Detail the file organization patterns including src/, android/, and ios/ directories. Explain how the application initializes, handles platform-specific configurations, and manages build processes. Include guidance on development workflow, debugging setup, and native module integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Express Server Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Express.js server configuration and initialization. Detail the server startup process, middleware stack configuration, and application bootstrapping. Document environment variable management, port configuration, and security headers setup. Explain the Express app instance creation, static asset serving, and CORS configuration. Cover error handling middleware, request logging, and health check endpoints. Include deployment configurations for different environments, SSL/TLS setup, and performance tuning parameters. Address server monitoring, graceful shutdown procedures, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user authentication system. Document the complete authentication flow including registration, login, profile management, and session handling. Explain the integration with Firebase authentication, token management, and secure session persistence. Detail the Redux slice implementation for state management, including authentication reducers, async thunks, and selector patterns. Cover the backend user controller implementation, user model schema, and middleware for authentication validation. Include security considerations, password hashing, JWT token handling, and session expiration. Document the user registration process, email verification workflows, and profile update mechanisms. Address common authentication scenarios, error handling, and user experience patterns for seamless authentication flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: BullMQ Queue System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the BullMQ queue system implementation in AITrendTracker. Document the queue configuration, connection management, and Redis integration patterns. Explain queue creation, job persistence strategies, and queue naming conventions. Cover queue monitoring, statistics collection, and health check mechanisms. Document queue scaling approaches, memory management, and performance optimization techniques. Include queue backup and recovery procedures, data serialization methods, and queue cleanup operations. Address queue security considerations, access controls, and monitoring dashboards. Provide troubleshooting guides for common queue issues, connection problems, and performance bottlenecks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for AITrendTracker's authentication endpoints. Document login, registration, logout, and password reset endpoints with complete request/response schemas. Specify authentication methods including JWT tokens, Firebase authentication integration, and session management. Include detailed error codes for invalid credentials, account verification requirements, and token expiration scenarios. Provide client implementation examples showing proper authentication flow, token refresh mechanisms, and secure storage practices. Document rate limiting policies for authentication attempts, security headers, and CORS configuration. Address common authentication issues, debugging techniques, and integration patterns with mobile applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: External API Integrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AITrendTracker's external API integration architecture. Document the Google Generative AI (Gemini) integration including model selection, API key management, and rate limiting strategies. Explain the AI service initialization patterns, graceful fallback mechanisms when API keys are unavailable, and error handling for external service failures. Cover the API response parsing, JSON validation, and data sanitization processes. Include configuration management for different environments, cost optimization techniques, and monitoring approaches. Document the AI chat functionality with conversational context management and conversation history handling. Address security considerations for API key storage and transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for AITrendTracker development environment setup. Document step-by-step installation procedures for both mobile and backend components. Include prerequisite requirements such as Node.js, React Native CLI, Android Studio, Xcode, and MongoDB setup. Provide detailed instructions for environment variable configuration, database seeding, and initial project setup. Cover development server startup procedures for both frontend and backend. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Document IDE setup recommendations and debugging configurations. Provide quick start examples for running the application locally and accessing the development dashboard.

wiki_catalogs.name: Redux Toolkit State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Redux Toolkit state management implementation. Thoroughly explain the store configuration, slice creation patterns, and entity adapter usage for efficient data normalization. Document each slice including authSlice for user authentication state, trendsSlice for trend data management, notificationsSlice for push notifications, predictionSlice for AI predictions, geoSlice for geographic data, and uiSlice for user interface state. Include practical examples of slice reducers, async thunks, selectors, and state updates. Explain the integration with React components through custom hooks and the overall data flow patterns. Address performance optimization techniques, state persistence strategies, and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: REST API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the backend services. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Detail the trend management APIs including CRUD operations, search functionality, and category filtering. Cover user management endpoints for authentication, profile management, and preferences. Document notification APIs for subscription management and alert delivery. Include AI chat endpoints for trend analysis and explainability features. Provide request/response examples, error codes, status codes, and rate limiting information. Address API versioning, pagination patterns, and data validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trend Discovery Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the trend discovery engine. Document the real-time trend feed implementation, including the home screen layout, trending screen functionality, and trend card components. Explain the trend data ingestion pipeline, real-time feed processing, and trend categorization logic. Detail the backend trend service implementation, MongoDB schema design for trend models, and personalization algorithms. Cover the feed list component architecture, infinite scrolling implementation, and trend filtering mechanisms. Document the trend scoring system, popularity metrics, and recommendation algorithms. Include the personalization service implementation for user-specific trend suggestions, preference-based filtering, and adaptive content delivery. Address performance optimization for real-time updates, caching strategies, and scalable data processing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Worker Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AITrendTracker's worker implementations and execution patterns. Document the worker registration process, lifecycle management, and concurrency control mechanisms. Explain worker-specific job processing logic, error handling strategies, and retry policies. Cover worker scaling configurations, resource allocation, and load balancing approaches. Document worker monitoring, performance metrics, and health checks. Include worker security measures, authentication requirements, and authorization patterns. Address worker debugging techniques, logging strategies, and performance profiling. Provide guidelines for adding new worker types, extending existing workers, and optimizing worker performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trend Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for AITrendTracker's trend management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for trends including creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion of trend records. Specify endpoints for trend categorization, trending status updates, and moderation workflows. Include request/response schemas for trend objects with scoring, confidence metrics, and geographic data. Document filtering options by category, location, time range, and popularity thresholds. Cover pagination strategies, sorting mechanisms, and search functionality. Provide examples of trend enrichment operations, batch processing endpoints, and administrative moderation tools. Address data validation rules, input sanitization, and trend lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confidence Scoring System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AITrendTracker's AI confidence scoring system. Explain the confidence score calculation methodology, ranging from 0-100 scale, and how it influences trend predictions and user interface display. Document the ConfidenceRing component implementation including visual representation, animation patterns, and interactive behaviors. Cover the scoring algorithm inputs including sentiment analysis, virality indicators, and historical trend patterns. Explain how confidence scores are stored in the database, updated during analysis, and used for trend prioritization. Include guidelines for interpreting confidence levels, threshold configurations, and score adjustment strategies. Address edge cases where confidence scores may be low or unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mobile Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the React Native mobile application component. Describe the high-level design patterns including Redux Toolkit state management, React Navigation for screen routing, and Socket.IO client for real-time communication. Document the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx through AuthNavigator to individual screen components. Explain the mobile-specific architectural decisions such as native module integration, platform-specific styling, and performance optimizations. Include data flow patterns from Redux store to UI components and real-time updates via WebSocket connections. Address mobile-specific concerns like offline functionality, push notifications, and device permissions. Document the theming system, responsive design patterns, and accessibility implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation and Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React Navigation system implementation. Explain the AuthNavigator setup for authentication flow, screen component organization, and navigation patterns throughout the application. Document each screen component including HomeScreen, LoginScreen, RegisterScreen, TrendingScreen, ProfileScreen, SearchScreen, NotificationsScreen, SavedScreen, and AIChatScreen. Detail navigation parameters, screen transitions, tab navigation, and stack navigation patterns. Include examples of programmatic navigation, deep linking, and navigation state management. Address navigation performance optimization, screen lifecycle management, and integration with Redux state.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database integration using MongoDB and Mongoose. Detail the schema design patterns, model definitions, and data relationships across all entities. Document the Trend model with scoring algorithms, confidence calculations, and temporal data tracking. Cover User model with authentication, preferences, and activity tracking. Explain Notification model for real-time alerts and user subscriptions. Detail TrendHistory model for trend evolution tracking and analytics. Document database indexing strategies, query optimization patterns, and performance considerations. Include Firebase Admin integration for cloud storage and authentication services. Address data migration strategies, backup procedures, and data consistency patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Intelligence Platform
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI intelligence platform. Document the AI-powered trend analysis implementation, including explainability components, confidence scoring systems, and platform trust indicators. Explain the integration with external AI APIs (OpenAI/Gemini), AI model deployment, and prediction algorithms. Detail the frontend AI components including confidence rings, relationship graphs, and intelligence badges. Cover the backend AI services including trend enhancement, analytics processing, and optimization algorithms. Document the confidence scoring methodology, explainability features, and trust matrix calculations. Include the relationship graph visualization, platform intelligence badges display, and AI-powered trend enrichment. Address AI model performance optimization, batch processing capabilities, and real-time inference patterns. Explain the AI moderation system, bias detection, and quality assurance mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Job Scheduling Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AITrendTracker's job scheduling and pipeline management system. Document job creation, scheduling strategies, and execution timing mechanisms. Explain job dependency management, priority queuing, and batch processing patterns. Cover job lifecycle stages, state transitions, and completion tracking. Document job validation, sanitization, and security checks. Include job monitoring, progress tracking, and status reporting mechanisms. Address job cancellation, suspension, and resumption capabilities. Provide guidelines for job performance optimization, resource management, and error recovery strategies. Document job audit trails, logging patterns, and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for user profile management endpoints. Document profile creation, update, and retrieval operations with complete user data schemas. Specify endpoints for preference management, notification settings, saved trends, and activity tracking. Include request/response examples for profile customization, privacy settings, and subscription management. Document user search and discovery endpoints with filtering capabilities and ranking algorithms. Cover profile picture upload, bio editing, and social integration features. Provide examples of user analytics endpoints, activity feeds, and recommendation systems. Address data protection requirements, GDPR compliance, and user consent management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Explainability Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AITrendTracker's AI explainability features. Document the AIExplainability component implementation including key drivers display, sentiment analysis explanations, and virality prediction reasoning. Explain how the AI service generates structured explanations with title/description pairs for trend drivers. Cover the explainability data format, validation processes, and frontend rendering patterns. Include guidelines for presenting AI-generated insights in user-friendly formats, handling incomplete explanations, and providing alternative explanations when AI output is limited. Document the relationship between explainability features and user trust building, including transparency mechanisms and bias mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Services Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Node.js/Express backend services. Describe the server-side architecture including Express.js configuration, middleware stack, and routing patterns. Document the service layer architecture with controllers, services, and data access patterns. Explain the database integration using Mongoose and MongoDB, including schema design and indexing strategies. Cover the real-time communication implementation using Socket.IO with Redis adapter for horizontal scaling. Document the background job processing system using BullMQ for asynchronous task management. Include API design patterns, error handling strategies, and security implementations. wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication Layer

Document how real-time updates are integrated with Redux state management, including socket event listeners, action dispatching, and state synchronization. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the geographic intelligence system. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AITrendTracker: Real-Time AI-Powered Trend Discovery Platform

## Project Introduction

AITrendTracker is a sophisticated mobile-first platform designed to provide users with real-time insights into trending topics across social media, news, and cultural phenomena. The application combines advanced AI analytics with real-time data streaming to deliver predictive intelligence about emerging trends, making it an essential tool for content creators, marketers, researchers, and curious users who want to stay ahead of the curve.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Real-Time Intelligence**: Deliver instant notifications about emerging trends through WebSocket-powered live feeds
- **AI-Powered Analysis**: Provide deep insights into trend behavior, virality patterns, and predictive analytics
- **Cross-Platform Intelligence**: Aggregate signals from multiple platforms to create comprehensive trend profiles
- **User-Centric Experience**: Offer intuitive discovery interfaces with personalized filtering and categorization
- **Scalable Architecture**: Build a robust backend capable of handling high-volume real-time data processing

### Target Audience

- Social media managers and digital marketers
- Content creators and influencers
- Researchers studying cultural phenomena
- Business analysts tracking market trends
- General users interested in staying informed about current events

## Technical Architecture

AITrendTracker employs a modern, scalable architecture that separates concerns between client-side presentation, real-time data streaming, and backend intelligence processing.

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Mobile Frontend"
        A[React Native App] --> B[Redux Toolkit Store]
        A --> C[Navigation System]
        A --> D[Real-time Socket Client]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        E[Express Server] --> F[MongoDB Database]
        E --> G[Redis Cache]
        E --> H[Socket.IO Server]
        E --> I[BullMQ Queues]
    end
    
    subgraph "AI/ML Layer"
        J[OpenAI/Gemini API] --> K[Analysis Engine]
        L[Geographic Engine] --> M[Pattern Recognition]
        N[Recommendation Engine] --> O[Prediction Models]
    end
    
    D --> H
    H --> E
    E --> J
    E --> L
    E --> N
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture follows a clear separation of concerns:

**Frontend Layer**: React Native-based mobile application with real-time capabilities
**Backend Layer**: Node.js/Express server handling API requests and business logic  
**Data Layer**: MongoDB for persistent storage with Redis for caching
**Real-time Layer**: Socket.IO for bidirectional communication
**AI Layer**: Integration with OpenAI and Google Gemini for intelligent analysis
**Processing Layer**: BullMQ for background job processing and task queuing

### Design Patterns

- **Redux Toolkit Pattern**: Centralized state management with slice-based organization
- **Entity Adapter Pattern**: Efficient data normalization and caching
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time event streaming via WebSocket connections
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service instantiation based on configuration
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable AI analysis engines and prediction models

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application bootstraps through several key entry points that establish the foundation for the entire system:

**Mobile Application Entry**
- [`App.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/App.tsx): Root component managing app lifecycle, socket connections, and global providers
- [`AuthNavigator.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/navigations/AuthNavigator.tsx): Navigation configuration for all application screens

**Backend Server Entry**
- [`server.js`](backend/server.js): Main server initialization with database connections, cron jobs, and worker setup
- [`app.js`](backend/src/app.js): Express application configuration with middleware and route registration

### Core Modules

**State Management Architecture**
- [`store/index.ts`](AITrendTracker7/src/store/index.ts): Central Redux store configuration with persistence setup
- [`store/slices/trendsSlice.ts`](AITrendTracker7/src/store/slices/trendsSlice.ts): Entity adapter-based trend management
- [`store/slices/predictionSlice.ts`](AITrendTracker7/src/store/slices/predictionSlice.ts): AI prediction state management

**Real-time Communication**
- [`src/services/socketService.ts`](AITrendTracker7/src/services/socketService.ts): WebSocket client with automatic reconnection and batching
- [`backend/src/services/socketService.js`](backend/src/services/socketService.js): Server-side WebSocket management

**Data Processing Pipeline**
- [`backend/src/services/trendService.js`](backend/src/services/trendService.js): Core trend data operations and queries
- [`backend/src/services/aiService.js`](backend/src/services/aiService.js): AI analysis and prediction generation

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- [`AITrendTracker7/src/utils/config.ts`](AITrendTracker7/src/utils/config.ts): Mobile app configuration constants
- [`backend/.env`](backend/.env): Backend service environment variables and API keys

**Build and Development**
- [`AITrendTracker7/package.json`](AITrendTracker7/package.json): Mobile app dependencies and scripts
- [`backend/package.json`](backend/package.json): Backend service dependencies and configurations

### External Dependencies

**Mobile Application Dependencies**
- React Native 0.84.1 with modern navigation patterns
- Redux Toolkit for state management with persistence
- Socket.IO client for real-time communication
- Firebase integration for authentication and cloud services
- MMKV for efficient local storage

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express.js for REST API development
- Mongoose for MongoDB object modeling
- Socket.IO for real-time bidirectional communication
- BullMQ for job queue processing
- Bull Board for queue monitoring dashboard
- Winston for structured logging

### Integration Points

**Real-time Data Flow**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Mobile Client
    participant Socket as Socket.IO Client
    participant Server as Backend Server
    participant DB as MongoDB
    participant AI as AI Service
    
    Client->>+Socket: Connect with auth token
    Socket->>+Server: WebSocket handshake
    Server->>+DB: Initialize connections
    Server->>+AI: Setup AI analysis pipeline
    Server-->>-Socket: Connection established
    Server-->>-Client: Welcome message
    
    Note over Server,AI: Background processing
    Server->>AI: Process new trend data
    AI-->>Server: Analysis results
    Server->>Socket: Emit real-time updates
    Socket->>Client: Push trend notifications
```

**Data Processing Pipeline**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Trend Data Source] --> B[Data Ingestion]
    B --> C[Preprocessing & Validation]
    C --> D[Geographic Analysis]
    D --> E[AI Enhancement]
    E --> F[Pattern Recognition]
    F --> G[Real-time Broadcasting]
    
    H[Background Jobs] --> I[Data Aggregation]
    I --> J[Historical Analysis]
    J --> K[Performance Optimization]
    
    G --> L[Client Updates]
    K --> M[Queue Processing]
    M --> N[Recurring Tasks]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Trend Discovery**
- Live trend notifications delivered via WebSocket connections
- Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff
- Batch processing to prevent UI thrashing during high-volume events
- Geographic trend mapping and heat visualization

**AI-Powered Intelligence**
- Multi-platform trust matrix analysis
- Bot detection and anomaly filtering
- Relationship network visualization
- Predictive confidence scoring system

**Advanced Filtering & Personalization**
- Category-based trend filtering
- Location-aware trend discovery
- User preference-based personalization
- Saved trends and bookmark management

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Optimizations**
- [`src/components/feed/FeedList.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/feed/FeedList.tsx): High-performance virtualized list rendering with FlashList
- [`src/components/common/ErrorBoundary.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/common/ErrorBoundary.tsx): Robust error handling with graceful degradation
- [`src/components/ui/PredictiveSkeletons.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/ui/PredictiveSkeletons.tsx): Intelligent loading states with predictive animations

**AI Explainability Interface**
- [`src/components/ai/AIExplainability.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/ai/AIExplainability.tsx): Interactive confidence visualization and platform trust analysis
- [`src/components/ai/ConfidenceRing.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/ai/ConfidenceRing.tsx): Animated confidence score display
- [`src/components/ai/RelationshipGraph.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/ai/RelationshipGraph.tsx): Visual relationship network mapping

**Geographic Intelligence**
- [`src/components/geo/TrendPropagationOverlay.tsx`](AITrendTracker7/src/components/geo/TrendPropagationOverlay.tsx): Heatmap visualization of geographic trend spread
- [`src/services/geoTrendEngine.js`](backend/src/services/geoTrendEngine.js): Advanced geographic pattern recognition and spike detection

### Feature Architecture

**State Management Flow**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> AppStart
    AppStart --> AuthCheck
    AuthCheck --> Navigation
    
    state Navigation {
        [*] --> Home
        Home --> TrendDetail
        Home --> Search
        Home --> Categories
        TrendDetail --> Home
        Search --> TrendDetail
        Categories --> TrendDetail
    }
    
    state RealtimeProcessing {
        [*] --> SocketConnect
        SocketConnect --> TrendUpdate
        TrendUpdate --> StateUpdate
        StateUpdate --> UIRefresh
        UIRefresh --> SocketConnect
    }
    
    Navigation --> RealtimeProcessing
    RealtimeProcessing --> [*]
```

**AI Analysis Pipeline**
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Raw Trend Data] --> B[Data Validation]
    B --> C[Platform Analysis]
    C --> D[Sentiment Scoring]
    D --> E[Virality Prediction]
    E --> F[Confidence Calculation]
    F --> G[Enhanced Profile]
    
    H[Historical Data] --> I[Pattern Matching]
    I --> J[Behavioral Analysis]
    J --> K[Prediction Engine]
    K --> L[Recommendation System]
    
    G --> M[Real-time Updates]
    L --> M
```

The AITrendTracker platform represents a comprehensive solution for real-time trend discovery and analysis, combining cutting-edge AI technologies with robust engineering practices to deliver a seamless user experience while maintaining scalability and reliability.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](AITrendTracker7/package.json)
- [server.js](backend/server.js)
- [App.tsx](AITrendTracker7/App.tsx)
- [AuthNavigator.tsx](AITrendTracker7/src/navigations/AuthNavigator.tsx)
- [socketService.ts](AITrendTracker7/src/services/socketService.ts)
- [index.ts](AITrendTracker7/src/store/index.ts)
- [trendsSlice.ts](AITrendTracker7/src/store/slices/trendsSlice.ts)
- [predictionSlice.ts](AITrendTracker7/src/store/slices/predictionSlice.ts)
- [app.js](backend/src/app.js)
- [trendService.js](backend/src/services/trendService.js)
- [AIExplainability.tsx](AITrendTracker7/src/components/ai/AIExplainability.tsx)
- [aiService.js](backend/src/services/aiService.js)
- [FeedList.tsx](AITrendTracker7/src/components/feed/FeedList.tsx)

</blog>
